main_paper.raw: {"methods":["CTNet: a hybrid CNN-Transformer network for 6D object pose estimation","Hierarchical Feature Extractor (HFE) enhancing C2f and ELAN modules for local feature extraction","CNN-based PointNet module for spatial feature extraction from point clouds","Transformer module for capturing global context and long-range dependencies","Fusion of local, spatial, and global features for pose estimation"],"problems":["Accurate 6D object pose estimation from RGB-D images","CNNs' inability to capture long-range dependencies and global context","High computational cost and redundancy in deep or dense fusion networks for pose estimation","Efficient pose estimation suitable for real-time and mobile computing platforms"],"datasets":["LineMOD","YCB-Video"],"metrics":["Accuracy","FLOPs (Floating Point Operations)"],"results":[{"metric":"FLOPs","value":"CTNet achieves nearly half the FLOPs of current methods on LineMOD and YCB-Video"},{"metric":"Accuracy","value":"CTNet achieves high accuracy on LineMOD and YCB-Video (exact numbers not specified in provided text)"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes CTNet, a novel hybrid network combining CNN and Transformer for 6D pose estimation, capturing both local and global features without deep networks","Introduces the Hierarchical Feature Extractor (HFE), an enhanced module for efficient and effective local feature extraction from RGB-D data","Integrates a CNN-based PointNet module for spatial feature extraction from point clouds within the hybrid architecture","Demonstrates that HFE is highly adaptable and transferable to other 6D pose estimation architectures","Achieves a balance between high accuracy and low computational cost, outperforming existing methods in efficiency"]}

methods: CTNet: a hybrid CNN-Transformer network for 6D object pose estimation
Hierarchical Feature Extractor (HFE) enhancing C2f and ELAN modules for local feature extraction
CNN-based PointNet module for spatial feature extraction from point clouds
Transformer module for capturing global context and long-range dependencies
Fusion of local, spatial, and global features for pose estimation
problems: Accurate 6D object pose estimation from RGB-D images
CNNs' inability to capture long-range dependencies and global context
High computational cost and redundancy in deep or dense fusion networks for pose estimation
Efficient pose estimation suitable for real-time and mobile computing platforms

results.value: CTNet achieves nearly half the FLOPs of current methods on LineMOD and YCB-Video
CTNet achieves high accuracy on LineMOD and YCB-Video (exact numbers not specified in provided text)

novelty_claims: Proposes CTNet, a novel hybrid network combining CNN and Transformer for 6D pose estimation, capturing both local and global features without deep networks
Introduces the Hierarchical Feature Extractor (HFE), an enhanced module for efficient and effective local feature extraction from RGB-D data
Integrates a CNN-based PointNet module for spatial feature extraction from point clouds within the hybrid architecture
Demonstrates that HFE is highly adaptable and transferable to other 6D pose estimation architectures
Achieves a balance between high accuracy and low computational cost, outperforming existing methods in efficiency

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Full Flow Bidirectional Fusion Network (FFB6D) for 6D pose estimation from a single RGBD image","Bidirectional fusion modules applied at each encoding and decoding layer to combine appearance (RGB) and geometry (depth/point cloud) information","SIFT-FPS: a 3D keypoint selection algorithm that considers both texture and geometry information for keypoint localization"],"problems":["6D pose estimation from a single RGBD image","How to fully leverage complementary appearance (RGB) and geometry (depth) information for 6D pose estimation","Improving representation learning for objects with similar appearance or reflective surfaces","Selecting salient and distinctive 3D keypoints for accurate pose estimation"],"datasets":["YCB-Video","LineMOD","Occlusion LineMOD"],"metrics":["6D pose estimation accuracy (ADD(-S) metric)","Average Distance of Model Points (ADD)","Average Distance of Model Points for Symmetric Objects (ADD-S)"],"results":[{"metric":"6D pose estimation accuracy (YCB-Video)","value":"Outperforms state-of-the-art by a large margin (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)"},{"metric":"6D pose estimation accuracy (LineMOD)","value":"Outperforms state-of-the-art by a large margin (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)"},{"metric":"6D pose estimation accuracy (Occlusion LineMOD)","value":"Outperforms state-of-the-art by a large margin (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a novel full flow bidirectional fusion network that fuses RGB and depth features at every encoding and decoding layer, rather than only at the end or via simple concatenation.","Introduces a 3D keypoint selection algorithm (SIFT-FPS) that leverages both texture and geometry information, improving keypoint localization for pose estimation.","Achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple 6D pose estimation benchmarks without requiring time-consuming post-refinement procedures."]}

novelty_claims: Proposes a novel full flow bidirectional fusion network that fuses RGB and depth features at every encoding and decoding layer, rather than only at the end or via simple concatenation.
Introduces a 3D keypoint selection algorithm (SIFT-FPS) that leverages both texture and geometry information, improving keypoint localization for pose estimation.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["DenseFusion framework: a heterogeneous architecture that processes RGB and depth data individually and fuses them at the pixel level via a dense fusion network to extract pixel-wise dense feature embeddings for pose estimation","End-to-end iterative pose refinement procedure integrated within the neural network architecture"],"problems":["6D object pose estimation from RGB-D images","Fully leveraging complementary RGB and depth data for pose estimation","Achieving accurate and real-time 6D pose estimation in cluttered and occluded scenes","Reducing reliance on costly and slow post-processing steps (e.g., ICP)"],"datasets":["YCB-Video","LineMOD"],"metrics":["Pose accuracy","Inference speed"],"results":[{"metric":"Pose accuracy improvement over PoseCNN+ICP","value":"3.5% higher"},{"metric":"Inference speed compared to PoseCNN+ICP","value":"200x faster"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a dense pixel-wise fusion of RGB and depth features for 6D pose estimation, enabling explicit reasoning about local appearance and geometry","Integrates an iterative pose refinement procedure directly into the end-to-end neural network, eliminating the need for slow post-processing steps like ICP","Achieves state-of-the-art accuracy and real-time inference speed, especially robust under heavy occlusion and cluttered scenes"]}

methods: Pix2Pose: a framework that predicts pixel-wise 3D coordinates of objects from RGB images using 3D models without textures
Auto-encoder architecture for estimating 3D coordinates and expected errors per pixel
Generative adversarial training to recover occluded parts of objects
Transformer loss: a novel loss function to handle symmetric objects by guiding predictions to the closest symmetric pose
Use of Perspective-n-Point (PnP) algorithm with RANSAC for pose computation from 2D-3D correspondences

Pose estimation without requiring textured 3D models

results.value: Pix2Pose outperforms state-of-the-art methods (exact values not provided in excerpt)
Pix2Pose outperforms state-of-the-art methods (exact values not provided in excerpt)

novelty_claims: Pix2Pose is a novel framework that regresses pixel-wise 3D coordinates from RGB images using 3D models without textures, enabling pose estimation without the need for textured models.
The method is robust to occlusion by leveraging generative adversarial training to recover occluded object parts.
A novel transformer loss is introduced to handle symmetric objects by guiding predictions to the closest symmetric pose, addressing the challenge of ambiguous views in symmetric objects.
Pix2Pose achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple benchmark datasets using only RGB images, even in the presence of occlusion and symmetry.

Learning rotation-invariant 3D geometric descriptors
Latent Fusion Attention Module for fusing 2D texture (RGB) and 3D geometric cues
Bridged Coarse-level Matching Loss leveraging RGB information to guide descriptor learning

novelty_claims: Proposes MatchU, a generic approach for 6D pose estimation of unseen objects using a Fuse-Describe-Match pipeline.
Introduces a Latent Fusion Attention Module to effectively combine RGB and 3D geometric information for descriptor learning.
Develops a Bridged Coarse-level Matching Loss that leverages RGB data to improve geometric descriptor learning.
Achieves rotation-invariant, color-aware 3D descriptors that generalize to unseen objects and capture symmetries without explicit symmetry annotations.
Surpasses existing methods in both accuracy and speed for unseen object 6D pose estimation, without requiring expensive retraining or rendering.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["SO-Pose: a deep neural network architecture that directly regresses 6D pose from a two-layer representation of 3D objects","Two-layer representation: combining 2D-3D correspondences (visible points) and self-occlusion information","Shared encoder with two decoders: one for 2D-3D correspondences, one for self-occlusion","Fusion of outputs from both decoders to regress 6DoF pose parameters","Cross-layer consistency losses to align self-occlusion, correspondence field, and 6D pose"],"problems":["Direct 6D pose estimation from a single RGB image in cluttered environments","Inferior accuracy of end-to-end 6D pose estimation methods compared to two-stage PnP/RANSAC-based approaches","Ambiguity and noise in correspondence fields for textureless objects leading to sub-optimal pose estimation","Lack of end-to-end trainability and differentiability in two-stage methods"],"datasets":["Not explicitly listed in the provided content, but references to 'various challenging datasets' and comparison to state-of-the-art methods suggest standard 6D pose estimation benchmarks such as LINEMOD, Occlusion LINEMOD, YCB-Video, etc."],"metrics":["Pose accuracy"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes SO-Pose, a novel deep architecture that directly regresses 6D pose from a two-layer representation of each 3D object","Introduces the use of self-occlusion information in addition to 2D-3D correspondences for pose estimation","Establishes a two-layer representation for each object in 3D space, enabling enforcement of cross-layer consistencies","Demonstrates that SO-Pose surpasses all other end-to-end competitors and achieves comparable accuracy to state-of-the-art two-stage methods while being much faster"]}

methods: SO-Pose: a deep neural network architecture that directly regresses 6D pose from a two-layer representation of 3D objects

Cross-layer consistency losses to align self-occlusion, correspondence field, and 6D pose
problems: Direct 6D pose estimation from a single RGB image in cluttered environments

novelty_claims: Proposes SO-Pose, a novel deep architecture that directly regresses 6D pose from a two-layer representation of each 3D object
Introduces the use of self-occlusion information in addition to 2D-3D correspondences for pose estimation
Establishes a two-layer representation for each object in 3D space, enabling enforcement of cross-layer consistencies
Demonstrates that SO-Pose surpasses all other end-to-end competitors and achieves comparable accuracy to state-of-the-art two-stage methods while being much faster

Incorporates ASPP to enrich semantic and boundary information for multiscale lesions.
Develops an innovative Attention Gate (AG) module as skip connections to facilitate context learning and focus on relevant features.
Demonstrates superior performance over pure CNN or Transformer-based methods (e.g., Attention U-Net, PVTFormer) on the LiTS dataset.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["ES6D: a computation efficient 6D pose regression framework","XYZNet: a fully convolutional feature extraction network for RGB-D data","Grouped Primitives (GP): a compact shape representation for handling symmetries","A(M)GPD: average (maximum) grouped primitives distance, a symmetry-invariant pose distance metric and loss function"],"problems":["Efficient and accurate 6D pose estimation of rigid objects from single RGB-D images","Handling symmetric objects in 6D pose regression, avoiding ambiguity in pose estimation","Reducing computational cost for real-time 6D pose estimation"],"datasets":["YCB-Video","T-LESS"],"metrics":["ADD-S (Average Distance of Model Points for Symmetric Objects)","A(M)GPD (Average/Maximum Grouped Primitives Distance)","Computation cost (inference speed)"],"results":[{"metric":"ADD-S (YCB-Video)","value":"substantially superior performance compared to prior works (exact numbers not provided in excerpt)"},{"metric":"ADD-S (T-LESS)","value":"demonstrates effectiveness, especially for symmetric objects (exact numbers not provided in excerpt)"},{"metric":"Computation cost","value":"low computational cost, suitable for real-time applications (exact numbers not provided in excerpt)"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes XYZNet, a fully convolutional and efficient feature extraction network for RGB-D data, improving both speed and accuracy for 6D pose estimation.","Introduces Grouped Primitives (GP) and the A(M)GPD loss, a novel symmetry-invariant pose distance metric that ensures all minima in the loss surface correspond to correct poses, effectively handling symmetric objects.","Provides a numerical simulation and visualization method to analyze the validity of the A(M)GPD loss, which can be applied to other 6D pose estimation frameworks.","Presents the ES6D framework, which achieves a superior trade-off between speed and accuracy on standard benchmarks."]}

methods: ES6D: a computation efficient 6D pose regression framework
XYZNet: a fully convolutional feature extraction network for RGB-D data
Grouped Primitives (GP): a compact shape representation for handling symmetries
A(M)GPD: average (maximum) grouped primitives distance, a symmetry-invariant pose distance metric and loss function
problems: Efficient and accurate 6D pose estimation of rigid objects from single RGB-D images
Handling symmetric objects in 6D pose regression, avoiding ambiguity in pose estimation
Reducing computational cost for real-time 6D pose estimation
metrics: ADD-S (Average Distance of Model Points for Symmetric Objects)

results.value: substantially superior performance compared to prior works (exact numbers not provided in excerpt)
demonstrates effectiveness, especially for symmetric objects (exact numbers not provided in excerpt)
low computational cost, suitable for real-time applications (exact numbers not provided in excerpt)

novelty_claims: Proposes XYZNet, a fully convolutional and efficient feature extraction network for RGB-D data, improving both speed and accuracy for 6D pose estimation.

Introduces Grouped Primitives (GP) and the A(M)GPD loss, a novel symmetry-invariant pose distance metric that ensures all minima in the loss surface correspond to correct poses, effectively handling symmetric objects.
Provides a numerical simulation and visualization method to analyze the validity of the A(M)GPD loss, which can be applied to other 6D pose estimation frameworks.
Presents the ES6D framework, which achieves a superior trade-off between speed and accuracy on standard benchmarks.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Pixel-wise Voting Network (PVNet): a network that predicts unit vectors from each pixel towards keypoints, enabling pixel-wise voting for keypoint localization via a RANSAC-based scheme","Vector-field representation for keypoint localization, allowing inference of invisible or truncated keypoints from visible parts","Uncertainty-driven Perspective-n-Point (PnP) algorithm that leverages spatial probability distributions of keypoint locations for robust pose estimation"],"problems":["6DoF pose estimation from a single RGB image, especially under severe occlusion and truncation","Robust localization of object keypoints when some are occluded or outside the image","Improving accuracy and robustness of pose estimation in cluttered and challenging environments"],"datasets":["LINEMOD","Occlusion LINEMOD","YCB-Video","Truncation LINEMOD (newly created for this work)"],"metrics":["ADD (Average Distance of Model Points)","Pose estimation accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"ADD (LINEMOD)","value":"86.3%"},{"metric":"ADD (Occlusion LINEMOD)","value":"40.8%"},{"metric":"ADD (previous state of the art, LINEMOD)","value":"79%"},{"metric":"ADD (previous state of the art, Occlusion LINEMOD)","value":"30.4%"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a novel pixel-wise voting network (PVNet) that predicts unit vectors from each pixel to keypoints, enabling robust keypoint localization even under occlusion and truncation","Introduces a vector-field representation for keypoint localization, which allows inferring locations of invisible or truncated keypoints from visible parts","Utilizes an uncertainty-driven PnP algorithm that leverages spatial probability distributions of keypoint locations for improved pose estimation accuracy","Demonstrates significant improvements over previous state-of-the-art methods on standard benchmarks","Creates a new Truncation LINEMOD dataset to evaluate robustness against truncation"]}

methods: Pixel-wise Voting Network (PVNet): a network that predicts unit vectors from each pixel towards keypoints, enabling pixel-wise voting for keypoint localization via a RANSAC-based scheme
Vector-field representation for keypoint localization, allowing inference of invisible or truncated keypoints from visible parts
Uncertainty-driven Perspective-n-Point (PnP) algorithm that leverages spatial probability distributions of keypoint locations for robust pose estimation
problems: 6DoF pose estimation from a single RGB image, especially under severe occlusion and truncation
Robust localization of object keypoints when some are occluded or outside the image

datasets: Truncation LINEMOD (newly created for this work)

results.metric: ADD (previous state of the art, LINEMOD)

novelty_claims: Proposes a novel pixel-wise voting network (PVNet) that predicts unit vectors from each pixel to keypoints, enabling robust keypoint localization even under occlusion and truncation
Introduces a vector-field representation for keypoint localization, which allows inferring locations of invisible or truncated keypoints from visible parts
Utilizes an uncertainty-driven PnP algorithm that leverages spatial probability distributions of keypoint locations for improved pose estimation accuracy
Demonstrates significant improvements over previous state-of-the-art methods on standard benchmarks
Creates a new Truncation LINEMOD dataset to evaluate robustness against truncation

Near real-time performance

novelty_claims: Proposes Attentional PointNet, a novel deep architecture that directly operates on sparse 3D points for object detection.
Extends visual attention mechanisms to 3D point clouds, enabling the network to learn where to look and attend to objects of interest, reducing the number of points processed and inference time.
Introduces a recurrent 3D Localization Network module for sequential region proposal in point clouds.
Achieves comparable detection accuracy and near real-time performance using only LiDAR data, without requiring multi-sensor fusion or 2D projections.
